industries.name: Medical Imaging & Radiotherapy
uses: Lutetium-177 (¹⁷⁷Lu, t½ = 6.65 days, beta + gamma emitter) is the fastest-growing therapeutic radioisotope — used in targeted radionuclide therapy (PRRT and PSMA-RLT) where lutetium is chelated to a targeting peptide that homes to cancer receptors

¹⁷⁷Lu-DOTATATE (Lutathera, Novartis): FDA and EMA approved for somatostatin receptor-positive gastroenteropancreatic neuroendocrine tumors (GEP-NETs) — the first FDA-approved radioligand therapy for NETs, 2018

¹⁷⁷Lu-PSMA-617 (Pluvicto, Novartis): FDA approved 2022 for metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC) — targets PSMA (prostate-specific membrane antigen) expressed on prostate cancer cells. Landmark VISION trial showed significant survival benefit

Lutetium-oxyorthosilicate (LSO, Lu₂SiO₅:Ce) and LYSO (Lu₁.₈Y₀.₂SiO₅:Ce) scintillator crystals: the dominant detector material in modern PET scanners — fast decay time (40ns), high density (7.4 g/cm³), and good energy resolution enable time-of-flight PET with exceptional image quality
¹⁷⁷Lu demand is growing explosively — Novartis's two approved radioligand therapies and a pipeline of 20+ clinical programs make lutetium one of the hottest critical materials in oncology

companies.name: Novartis (Advanced Accelerator Applications)
companies.use: Dominant radioligand therapy company. Markets Lutathera (¹⁷⁷Lu-DOTATATE) for neuroendocrine tumors and Pluvicto (¹⁷⁷Lu-PSMA-617) for prostate cancer — two blockbuster drugs driving unprecedented lutetium demand growth. Building dedicated lutetium production infrastructure.

companies.name: Eckert & Ziegler
companies.use: Major ¹⁷⁷Lu isotope producer and radiopharmaceutical manufacturer. Supplies carrier-free (no-carrier-added, n.c.a.) ¹⁷⁷Lu to Novartis and other radioligand therapy developers. Also produces ¹⁷⁷Lu-labeled therapeutic compounds for clinical programs.

companies.name: Siemens Healthineers
companies.use: Major PET scanner manufacturer and major LYSO scintillator crystal consumer. Its Biograph Vision PET/CT and Biograph Vision Quadra (the world's longest axial FOV PET scanner) use LYSO scintillator crystals containing lutetium.

companies.name: Saint-Gobain Crystals
companies.use: Major LSO and LYSO scintillator crystal grower for PET detector modules. Supplies lutetium oxyorthosilicate crystals to Siemens, GE, and Philips for incorporation into PET scanner detector rings.

industries.name: Petroleum Refining & Catalysis
uses: Lutetium in FCC (fluid catalytic cracking) catalyst formulations as a rare earth stabilizer — lutetium oxide improves zeolite thermal stability and vanadium passivation in high-performance FCC catalysts used in petroleum refining

Lutetium-based Lewis acid catalysts for organic synthesis — Lu³⁺ is a strong Lewis acid used in Diels-Alder reactions, Friedel-Crafts alkylations, and other carbon-carbon bond forming reactions in fine chemical synthesis
Lutetium alkoxide and lutetium trifluoromethanesulfonate (Lu(OTf)₃) as water-stable Lewis acid catalysts for asymmetric synthesis and polymerization reactions

companies.name: Grace (W.R. Grace)
companies.use: Major FCC catalyst manufacturer incorporating rare earth oxides including lutetium in premium FCC catalyst formulations for petroleum refinery applications requiring maximum thermal stability and catalytic activity.

industries.name: Specialty Materials
uses: Lutetium aluminum garnet (LuAG:Ce, Lu₃Al₅O₁₂:Ce) scintillator for high-energy physics detectors and medical CT — faster decay than YAG:Ce with higher density for better gamma stopping power
Lutetium gallium garnet (LGG) as substrate for epitaxial growth of magnetic garnet films in magneto-optical research
Lutetium is the heaviest and most expensive stable lanthanide — used in specialty applications where its specific nuclear or optical properties justify the premium over lighter rare earths

companies.name: Crytur
companies.use: Major producer of LuAG:Ce, YAG:Ce, and other garnet scintillator crystals for high-energy physics (CERN CMS detector), medical CT, and electron microscopy detector applications.
